title: Union with multiple binds produces unexpected results
description:
Virtuoso Version: 07.10.3209
select distinct ?direct ?link where {

    ?0 a <tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#Office> .

    {

        ?0 ?link [] bind (true as ?direct)

    } union {

        [] ?link ?0 bind (false as ?direct)

    }

}

Expected Results
direct  link
true    http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type
true    http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#area
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#city
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#code
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#country
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#employee
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#state
false   tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#office

Actual Results
direct  link
        http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type
        http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#area
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#city
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#code
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#country
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#employee
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#state
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#office


Fine without union.
Likely related to #212 and #252.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE office_data (id Int32, name String, location String, active UInt8);
INSERT INTO office_data (id, name, location, active) VALUES (1, 'Main Office', 'New York', 1);
INSERT INTO office_data (id, name, location, active) VALUES (2, 'Branch Office', 'Los Angeles', 0);

SELECT DISTINCT if(active = 1, 'true', 'false') AS direct, location AS link FROM office_data WHERE active = 1 UNION ALL SELECT DISTINCT if(active = 1, 'true', 'false') AS direct, location AS link FROM office_data WHERE active = 0;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM office_data WHERE location IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Union with multiple binds in query produces unexpected results
